items.title: Alberta trucking company at centre of fatal Manitoba crash has spotty record, records show
items.summary_auto: A trucking company at the centre of a deadly Brandon, Man., crash has a spotty record in Alberta, where it's been registered since having its safety permit pulled in Manitoba over four years ago.

items.title: Family sues N.L. government and group home, claiming wrongful death of Tony Humby complainant
items.summary_auto: The family of a complainant who died following his testimony at Tony Humby's trial has filed a lawsuit alleging the police, the province and a group home in the care of the Newfoundland and Labrador government were negligent in their duty of care, which allegedly contributed to his death.

items.title: TD Bank holds customer responsible for $15K loss, won't say how account hacking ruled out
Anatomy of a fraud: How scammers use our emotions against us
items.summary_auto: While frauds continue to evolve, experts say the emotional manipulation behind them largely stays the same — so knowing how those pressure tactics work and paying attention to how you’re feeling could be the key to avoiding being scammed.

items.title: CUSMA a blessing for some Canadian businesses, a pain for others despite escaping U.S. tariffs
items.summary_auto: Canadian businesses that comply with CUSMA were spared in the Trump administration's latest tariffs, but for many of them, it hasn't brought a total sigh of relief as compliance has come with its own set of frustrations. "It's actually quite a nightmare," says the co-owner of a puzzle company in Ottawa.

items.title: Facebook is paying people overseas promoting Alberta separatism
items.summary_auto: A CBC News investigation found a number of Facebook accounts run by people overseas impersonating real Albertan separatists.

items.title: Constitution, energy drinks, electoral map: High-stakes bills caught in Quebec’s legislative rush
items.summary_auto: Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette's government is entering the final sprint at the National Assembly, with a logjam of more than a dozen bills only days before the summer break.

items.title: #TheMoment he made a working pinball machine
items.summary_auto: Marco Bucci tells The National about the moment he made his own working pinball machine in Pickering, Ont.

items.title: Scott Pelley alleges political bias at CBS in post-firing interview
items.summary_auto: In his first interview since being fired from 60 Minutes, veteran journalist Scott Pelley detailed his allegations of political interference and bias from CBS management.

items.title: Iran says it's done with latest round of strikes, but warns Israel to stop attacking Lebanon
items.summary_auto: Iran's military announced on Monday that its first wave ​of attacks on Israel since a ceasefire in April was now over, although ‌it threatened to resume the strikes if Israel continued its attacks on Lebanon.

items.title: Philippines earthquake leads to landslides, raising death toll to at least 32
items.summary_auto: Newly reported casualties raised the overall death toll in the strongest earthquake to hit the Philippine archipelago this year to at least 32, officials said Monday, a magnitude 7.8 earthquake that triggered tsunami warnings for several hours.

items.title: Manslaughter charge laid in Thunder Bay, Ont., in connection with missing persons case
items.summary_auto: A 33-year-old man from Thunder Bay, Ont., has been charged with manslaughter in connection with the death of 36-year-old Kelsey Anderson, who was reported missing in the northwestern Ontario city last month. The Thunder Bay Police Service (TBPS) said the charge is the result of a major crime unit investigation, during which a deceased person, later identified as Anderson, was located.

items.title: Armenia's pro-West PM declares win as parliamentary vote tallying continues
Armenia PM declares 'historic victory' as vote tallying continues in parliamentary vote
items.summary_auto: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan declared victory early Monday in a parliamentary election after results from just over one-fifth of ‌polling sites showed his party with about 54 per cent of the vote.

items.title: Vancouver ranked best World Cup host city by Sports Illustrated
items.summary_auto: Sports Illustrated magazine says Vancouver is the best of 16 cities hosting World Cup matches, citing transportation, walkability and mild weather for topping its ranking list released this month.

items.title: Danish soccer player Christian Eriksen recovering in hospital after again collapsing during match
items.summary_auto: Christian Eriksen is "in good spirits" and expected to leave the hospital soon after collapsing on the field again playing for Denmark, the national team's physician said Monday.

items.title: 2 Virginia residents sue to stop UFC fight at White House for Trump's 80th birthday
items.summary_auto: A U.S. federal lawsuit seeks to halt the upcoming UFC fight card on the White House South Lawn in a mixed martial arts show timed for President Donald Trump's 80th birthday and part of the celebration of the nation's 250th anniversary.
